exercises.notes: Both bands all 3 sets. Set 3 reps fell off. **Jacob:** green band only, 10 × 10 × 10
**−5 kg vs 4/21 top set** (was 45 × 10/8 + 40 × 12). Reps clean across. **Jacob:** 25 kg × 12 × 3
Plate-loaded, seated, thigh pads. Held vs 4/21. **Jacob:** same weight
Iso-lateral seated row. Held vs 4/21. **Jacob:** 40 kg × 15 × 3

session_notes:
Fasted training (6am, eating window starts at noon).
**Pull-up volume bumped to 3 sets** (was 2 sets on 4/21). Reps degraded on set 3 (10 → 8 → 6) — typical fatigue curve adding a third set. Bands held at green + purple. Jacob still on green only (10×10×10) — wider gap to close on bodyweight pulling.
**Lat pulldown regressed 5 kg** vs 4/21's top set (45 → 40), but rep quality cleaned up: 12/12/12 across all 3 sets vs 4/21's 10/8 + drop to 40. Net work probably similar; this is a consolidation session, not a strength loss.
**Iso-lateral high row + plate-loaded row held identical to 4/21.** Two sessions at this load on both — ready for a bump next pull day.
**Barbell biceps curls cleaned up:** 10/10/10 across vs 4/21's 8/8/7. Same load, better execution. Likely less pre-fatigue than 4/21 because pulldown was lighter today (40 vs 45 kg). Per [[project_pt_programming_style|PT pattern]], curl numbers are still post-fatigue from pulldown + rows — but the cleaner reps suggest curls have room when pulldown isn't maxed.
**Still no face pulls / no rear delt work.** Two pull days in a row (4/21, 4/28) with zero direct rear delt volume. Shoulder imbalance flag stands — rear delts are getting indirect row work only. **Raise with PT for next pull day.**
**No ring row warmup today** (was used 4/21). Pull-up went straight in cold (after general warmup).
